behaviors.description: The primary translator produces a grammatically coherent translation that preserves the meaning of the source text, not just a word-for-word substitution.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP triggers: correctness depends on real model output quality (grammar + meaning preservation), so stubs would hide real failures. MOCK-HIDE: a mocked translator can always return a perfect fixture and cannot detect semantic/fluency regressions. BIDIRECTIONAL-REJECTION-GATE: rejected adjacent lower level System because the behavior text targets output quality ("grammatically coherent" / "preserves the meaning"), not HTTP/OS failure modes; rejected adjacent higher level Workflow because the behavior is a single-step outcome ("The primary translator produces...") with no multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run real NMT API translations on a curated multilingual set and score semantic fidelity/fluency with human rubric or validated reference checks; fail if coherence/meaning thresholds are not met.

behaviors.description: When the translation API returns a 503 error, the service transparently falls back to the dictionary-based translator without returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (API client error -> fallback translator -> caller response), not single-function logic. REAL-INFRA does not require real network; an in-process stubbed 503 at the client boundary is sufficient. INTEGRATION-BRAKE checks pass: no required real OS/network behavior, no dependence on model output quality, no multi-step agent pipeline. BIDIRECTIONAL-REJECTION-GATE: rejected adjacent lower Unit because behavior explicitly spans modules ("falls back ... without returning an error to the caller"); rejected adjacent higher System because only status handling is required ("returns a 503 error"), reproducible without real HTTP transport.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service + API client stub + SQLite-backed fallback translator; force 503 from primary client and assert successful fallback response (no propagated error) to caller.

behaviors.description: The dictionary-based fallback returns the correct pre-stored translation for phrases that exist in the SQLite database.
behaviors.justification: Behavior boundary is repository/storage interaction: query + mapping from SQLite to fallback output. Unit is too low because the real failure can be in SQL/schema/wiring, not pure function logic. System is not required: local SQLite can be exercised in-process without OS/network/subprocess effects. BIDIRECTIONAL-REJECTION-GATE: rejected adjacent lower Unit due explicit DB dependency ("exist in the SQLite database"); rejected adjacent higher System because behavior does not require external infrastructure beyond local module+DB wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Use test SQLite DB with seeded phrase rows; call fallback translator and verify exact stored translation is returned for existing phrases.

behaviors.description: The quality tracking comparison correctly computes edit distance between primary and fallback outputs and stores the metric.
behaviors.justification: Computation plus persistence crosses module boundaries (comparison logic -> metrics storage). A pure Unit test could validate distance math but miss storage wiring failures; Integration is minimum to catch real failure described. No real OS/network/subprocess is required, and no AI-output-quality judgment is required. BIDIRECTIONAL-REJECTION-GATE: rejected adjacent lower Unit because behavior includes persistence ("and stores the metric"); rejected adjacent higher System because failure mode is local computation/storage wiring, reproducible in-process.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke quality tracker with fixed primary/fallback strings, assert expected character edit distance, and verify persisted metric record contents in test DB.

behaviors.description: When both translators are available, the service returns the primary translation and logs the quality comparison in the background.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration/state handoff across components (primary selection + async quality logging), so Unit is too low. Agentic is not required because correctness here is selection/logging behavior, not judging model output quality. System is not required unless real process/network timing primitives are essential; behavior text does not require that. BIDIRECTIONAL-REJECTION-GATE: rejected adjacent lower Unit due multi-module async coordination ("returns the primary translation and logs ... in the background"); rejected adjacent higher System because no explicit real OS/subprocess/network condition is part of the requirement.
behaviors.test_description: With both translators stubbed and metrics store enabled, call service and assert immediate response equals primary output; then await background task completion and assert quality log entry was written.
